mechanism_narrative: A lower FEDFUNDS path reduces discount rates and financial stress, so when policy stays near the effective lower bound the S&P 500 should rise over the next 20 trading days as valuation multiples expand.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive on average when FEDFUNDS is near its lower bound, or if the signal's rolling 2-year Rank IC falls below 0.03, reject the mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent baseline signals weakening household income and tighter cyclical growth expectations, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average forward return is not below the unconditional sample mean.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth outpaces payroll growth, demand is accelerating faster than labor supply, implying stronger near-term revenue momentum for cyclicals and a positive S&P 500 response over the next week.
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term RSAFS growth minus PAYEMS growth does not outperform each series alone in sign prediction, or if the 5-day hit rate is below 55% in expanding-demand periods.

mechanism_narrative: An improvement in INDPRO with no concurrent rise in unemployment indicates stronger real activity without labor-market deterioration, supporting earnings revisions and higher S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO increases month-over-month while UNRATE is flat or lower
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition fails to produce positive excess 20-day returns relative to all months with INDPRO gains, or if the conditional Rank IC remains below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A faster CPIAUCSL rise with a lagging FEDFUNDS rate widens the policy-real-rate gap, forcing repricing of discount rates and hurting S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains below the implied inflation trend
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-acceleration episodes followed by unchanged policy do not produce negative 20-day returns more often than not, or if the interaction signal's AUC stays below 0.52.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in GDPC1 indicates shrinking aggregate demand and weaker corporate revenue expectations, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDP contractions do not precede below-median 60-day equity returns, or if the sign accuracy drops below 55% in recession-adjacent windows.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls together, labor income growth and consumer spending capacity improve, creating a positive short-run tailwind for S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases over 1 month and UNRATE declines over the same window
failure_condition: Reject if the joint payrolls-unemployment improvement condition does not outperform either proxy alone, or if triggered 5-day returns are not positive in at least 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST after prior strength signals tightening real-economy activity and weaker construction-linked demand, which should lower S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 10% from its 6-month average while INDPRO is flat or weaker
failure_condition: Reject if large housing declines fail to precede negative 60-day equity returns, or if the conditional hit rate is not significantly below 50% across rolling evaluation windows.
